evidence_notes: Global democratic legitimacy is under broad strain, with continuing freedom decline and widespread governance distrust.
Russia-Ukraine war and wider Middle East instability create simultaneous territorial, security, and energy-system stress.
The global system still has meaningful resilience tools, but adaptation finance and coordination remain insufficient.
Growing bloc polarization and widespread political fragmentation indicate moderate systemic division.
Energy and shipping disruptions are pushing up supply-chain risk and global input costs.
Multiple active wars with cross-border spillovers keep global conflict pressure high.
The global economy remains relatively resilient overall, though fiscal buffers face pressure from geopolitical shocks.
High forced displacement and persistent social strain support a moderate global urban/social stress score.
Institutions are responding, but slow adaptation and uneven coordination suggest moderate rigidity.
Large-scale hunger and acute food insecurity remain significant at the global level.
Current energy shocks show continued dependence on strategic fossil-fuel flows and chokepoints.
Record heat, rising climate disruption, and growing water stress support a high environmental-stress score.
